description: Rust port of psych::fa and FactoMineR::FAMD: exploratory factor analysis on numeric data, factor analysis of mixed data, and a standalone correlation-matrix builder covering the full psych::fa(cor=...) method surface.
release_notes:
Output restructuring, clearer errors, rewritten help.

- **One sheet per batch, k stacked inside.** The xlsx layout no longer fans out to one pair of sheets per `(batch, n_factors)`. Each `fa` batch now emits exactly `<batch>_sum` and `<batch>_load`; each `famd` batch emits `<batch>_sum`, `<batch>_vars`, and (when categoricals exist) `<batch>_cats`. Every result row carries its own `n_factors` column so Excel filters / pivots work directly. Loading and coord/contrib/cosÂ² columns widen to the batch's largest k; rows with smaller k leave the higher-index columns blank.
- **Real failure reasons in error messages.** `fa` and `famd` now collect per-job errors and surface the first three in the top-level error when zero batches succeed, instead of returning the generic `min_count`/`max_count` hint. Typical case: `cor = "tetrachoric"` with meta that leaves `type` unset now returns a clear per-pair type-mismatch error rather than the misleading "batch sizes" message.
- **Help pages rewritten.** Argument descriptions no longer cram full enum lists into the description column. The valid values (and implementation status) for `cor`, `fm`, `rotate`, and `method` are called out in dedicated Notes entries. Each command's help now lists the exact sheets and columns it produces, under `Examples` entries titled `Output: ...`.
